verses.text: Made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood — 5 by 5 cubits foursquare, 3 high. Horns at four corners, of the same piece, overlaid with brass. Pots, shovels, basins, fleshhooks, firepans — brass. Brass grate of network under the altar's compass. Four brass rings for staves. Shittim staves overlaid with brass. Hollow with boards. Guidance: The first piece met in the court — bronze takes the fire that flesh deserved. Hebrews 13 says: we have an altar. The cross stands in this same line.

He made the laver of brass, and its foot of brass, of the lookingglasses of the women assembling at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. Guidance: The women's bronze mirrors were melted down to make the laver. They gave up the surfaces they used to see themselves, so the priests would have a surface to be cleansed by. Sacrificial vanity becomes a means of grace.

The court — south fine-linen hangings 100 cubits, twenty pillars, brass sockets, silver hooks. Same north. West 50, ten pillars. East 50 — fifteen-cubit hangings each side, three pillars; gate 20 cubits of blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, needlework, four pillars. Pillars filleted with silver. 100 by 50, 5 high. Sockets brass, pins brass. Guidance: A fenced courtyard with one wide gate, embroidered in priestly colors. The boundary is firm; the way in is generous. God's house is enclosed, its door is open.

The sum of the tabernacle of testimony, counted by commandment of Moses for the Levites by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron. Bezaleel of Judah made all the LORD commanded. With him Aholiab of Dan — engraver, cunning workman, embroiderer in blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen. Guidance: Names matter. The builders are remembered with their fathers and tribes. God's house gets credited to the people who actually showed up to make it.

All gold: 29 talents, 730 shekels. Silver of the numbered congregation: 100 talents, 1,775 shekels — a bekah a head (603,550 men). 100 talents cast 100 sockets, one talent each. The 1,775 shekels made hooks, overlaid chapiters, filleted them. Brass: 70 talents, 2,400 shekels — door sockets, brass altar with grate, court sockets, pins. Guidance: A full audit. The God who counted Israel's first half-shekel ransom counts every piece of metal in His house. Nothing of His is unaccounted.
